channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: OpenAI says India court can’t hear case on alleged copyright breach
item.description: OpenAI has told an Indian court that any order to remove training data powering its ChatGPT service would be inconsistent with its legal obligations in the United States, according to a recent filing seen by Reuters.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: Samsung aims to turn its next generation of Galaxy smartphones into AI companions
item.description: Samsung is injecting another dose of artificial intelligence into the next version of its Galaxy smartphones, escalating an effort to simplify people’s lives while deepening their dependence on a device that accompanies them almost everywhere.
item.dc:creator: Michael Liedtke, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump administration shuts down White House Spanish-language page and social media
item.description: Within hours of President Donald Trump’s inauguration, the new administration took down the Spanish-language version of the official White House website. The site — currently https://www.whitehouse.gov/es/ — now gives users an “Error 404” message.
item.dc:creator: FERNANDA FIGUEROA, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: MrBeast not quite in the bidding race for TikTok just yet, rep says
item.description: Jimmy Donaldson — better known online as MrBeast — isn’t in the TikTok bidding race just yet, according to a representative for the YouTube star.
item.dc:creator: James Pollard, Haleluya Hadero And Wyatte Grantham-Philips, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Your Samsung Galaxy Ring might be the key to a smarter home thanks to SmartThings' connected device roadmap
item.description: At an exclusive VP roundtable during CES, I learned more about Samsung’s plans SmartThings - including how devices like the Samsung Galaxy Ring might fit in
item.dc:creator: josephine.watson@futurenet.com (Josephine Watson), TechRadar

item.title: Trump faces crypto world backlash after launch of meme coin
item.description: President Trump is facing backlash after he and first lady Melania Trump launched two cryptocurrency tokens just days before his inauguration.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Free updates are coming to Kingdom Come: Deliverance 2 after launch, including the ability to get a haircut
item.description: New Kingdom Come: Deliverance 2 post launch roadmap details the expansion content and free updates coming to the game.
item.dc:creator: dash.wood@futurenet.com (Dashiell Wood), TechRadar

item.title: The critical role of delivering reliable connectivity for thriving businesses
item.description: Discover how businesses can transform their network infrastructure for secure and reliable connectivity.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Gen AI ROI falls short of expectations, but belief persists
item.description: AI, and gen AI in particular, are continuing to bombard the enterprise, but the gains to date haven’t been as big, nor come as quickly, as many business leaders hoped.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Is Elon Musk a government employee? Here’s why it matters
item.description: Elon Musk, the world’s richest man, is CEO of Tesla and SpaceX, but when it comes to his work with the federal government, his status is a lot less clear.
item.dc:creator: Chris Morris, Fast Company

item.title: Trump highlights partnership investing $500 billion in AI
item.description: President Donald Trump is talking up a joint venture investing up to $500 billion for infrastructure tied to artificial intelligence by a new partnership formed by OpenAI, Oracle and SoftBank.
item.dc:creator: Josh Boak And Zeke Miller, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Sustainability, grid demands, AI workloads will challenge data center growth in 2025
item.description: Data centers this year will face several challenges as the demand for artificial intelligence introduces an evolution in AI hardware, on-premises and cloud-based strategies for training and inference, and innovations in power distributions—all while opposition to new data center developments continu
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Auto-followed Trump on Facebook? It's probably because you previously liked the POTUS account
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Some Facebook and Instagram users were surprised to find themselves automatically following the accounts of President Donald Trump or Vice President JD Vance this week after the president was inaugurated — but that's likely because they had previously followed official U.S.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: The bitter lesson for generative AI adoption
item.description: The rapid development and improvement in generative AI technology pose a challenge for training and fine-tuning as a sustainable path for adoption. If organizations constantly need to fine-tune new models for specific tasks, they might be in a costly cycle of catching up with new technology.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: The AI security tsunami
item.description: AI isn‘t just changing cloud computing, it’s completely reshaping how we think about security in the cloud—on both sides. Attackers use AI to cause breaches, and enterprises use AI to defend against such attacks. Let me break this down into what‘s happening and what enterprises need to do about it.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Trump orders government not to infringe on Americans’ speech, calls for censorship investigation
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — President Donald Trump on Monday ordered that no federal officer, employee or agent may unconstitutionally abridge the free speech of any American citizen, an early step toward his campaign promise to dismantle what he called government “censorship” of U.S. citizens.
item.dc:creator: ALI SWENSON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: How to manage photos, videos on new laptop with limited space
item.description: With limited hard drive space, tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson offers these tips for storing large video files and photos.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: JDK 24: The new features in Java 24
item.description: Java Development Kit (JDK) 24 is now in a second rampdown phase, with the feature set frozen at 24 features, ranging from a class-file API to a fourth preview of structured concurrency.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Army Prepares New AI Guidance Amid Cost Concerns | Federal News Network
item.description: The US Army is preparing new guidance on the use of artificial intelligence, focusing on cost considerations and commercial cloud services.
item.dc:creator: Jared Serbu, Federal News Network

item.title: TikTok refugees are pouring to Xiaohongshu. Here's what you need to know about the RedNote app
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — As the fate of TikTok hangs in the balance, U.S. TikTok users are flocking to the Chinese social media app Xiaohongshu, also called RedNote – making it the top downloaded app in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: FU TING and DAVID COHEN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Who could buy TikTok? Only a few parties are serious about offering
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The supremely popular TikTok could be banned on Jan. 19 under a federal law that forces the video sharing platform to divest itself from its China-based parent company, ByteDance, or shut down its U.S. operations.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: These mistakes could tank your credit score
item.description: Even small mistakes can negatively impact your credit score; here are some of the most common credit report errors and how you can fix them.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: How Apple is in the race for workplace AI
item.description: In a few years, every new employee entering the workforce will already have become accustomed to using AI to solve problems and help with tasks – and they’re going to want the same tools at work as those they use at home.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Bitcoin soars past $100,000 ahead of possible early action on crypto by Trump
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The price of bitcoin topped $100,000 again early Friday as a pumped up cryptocurrency industry expects early action by Donald Trump when he's sworn in as president next week.
item.dc:creator: ALAN SUDERMAN, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Python eats the world
item.description: In this week’s Python Report: Everyone’s favorite snake-named language is getting closer to being everyone’s favorite language, period. We also have a guide to unpacking the power of Python’s abstract base classes, and a tutorial for developers who want to use Cython to speed up their Python code.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Soft robotic armband gives prosthetic hand users natural control
item.description: Researchers found in a study that users of a robotic prosthetic hand could successfully grasp and move two objects at once without dropping them.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Taiwanese chipmaker TSMC posts 57% surge in profit thanks to the AI boom
item.description: HONG KONG (AP) — Taiwan computer chip maker TSMC reported Thursday that its profit in the last quarter rose 57%, buoyed by the artificial intelligence boom. The world’s biggest semiconductor manufacturer reported a net profit of 374.7 billion new Taiwan dollars ($11.4 billion), higher than expected.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC
